===''Pokémon the Series''=== ===={{aniseries|DP}}==== [[File:Mars anime.png|thumb|220px|Mars in {{aniseries|DP}}]] Mars debuted in ''[[DP096|Losing Its Lustrous!]]''. She, along with fellow {{tc|Commander}} [[Saturn]], attempted to steal the [[Lustrous Orb]] from the [[Celestic Town Historical Research Center]]. She also appeared in ''[[DP097|Double Team Turnover!]]'', in which she informed [[Cyrus]] that the [[Red Chain]] project was nearing completion. In ''[[DP110|Steeling Peace of Mind!]]'', Mars and her {{TP|Mars|Purugly}} brought the [[Spear Key]] to [[Iron Island]], where she activated the Iron Ruins to find the exact location of the [[Spear Pillar]] on [[Mt. Coronet]]. After failing to complete the task in ''[[DP111|Saving the World From Ruins!]]'', she attempted to blow up the island on Cyrus's orders, but she failed due to [[Riley]] and his {{TP|Riley|Lucario}} using [[Aura]] to contain the explosion. As a result, she stood down from field duties while Saturn was tasked with locating Spear Pillar. She later appeared in ''[[DP151|The Needs of the Three!]]'' and ''[[DP152|The Battle Finale of Legend!]]''. After the disappearance of Cyrus in the latter episode, she became distraught and even tried following him, but was stopped by Saturn. The duo, along with Jupiter, were later arrested. She appeared briefly in ''[[DP191|Memories are Made of Bliss!]]'' in {{TRT}}'s fantasy with all the other members of [[Team Galactic]]. ===={{aniseries|JN}}==== Mars reappeared in ''[[JNS01|The Arceus Chronicles (Part 1)]]'' through ''[[JNS04|The Arceus Chronicles (Part 4)]]'', taking part in Team Galactic's plan to bring back Cyrus. For this purpose, Team Galactic acquired {{p|Arceus}}'s [[Flame Plate]] and a {{p|Heatran}}, which were meant to power a device that would open a gate to the world that Cyrus had disappeared to. The operation, however, experienced a setback when the energy cannon exploded and Heatran went on a rampage. Mars and the other Commanders later faced Ash and his friends in order to stop them from interfering with them, but they were ultimately defeated and captured by {{an|Cynthia}} and later arrested by [[Officer Jenny]], ending their plan in a failure. ====Character==== Mars lacks the vindictive nature seen in her fellow Commanders. She was extremely loyal to [[Team Galactic]]'s cause and only acted in accordance with [[Cyrus]]'s direct orders. ====Pokémon==== {{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}} This listing is of Mars's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{TrainerPoké |main=yes |trainer=Mars |pkmn=Purugly |type1=Normal |img=Mars Purugly.png |epnum=DP097 |epname=Double Team Turnover! |vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima |vaen= |desc=Purugly's first appearance was alongside Mars in ''[[DP096|Losing Its Lustrous!]]'' as she was destroying the [[Celestic Town|Celestic Ruins]] to create a distraction so that Saturn could steal the Lustrous Orb. Purugly made a more significant appearance in [[DP097|the next episode]], in which it battled {{Ash}}, {{an|Dawn}}, and {{an|Cynthia}} for the orb and proved itself to be very powerful.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Mars |pkmn=Bronzor |type1=Steel |type2=Psychic |img=Mars Bronzor.png |epnum=DP097 |epname=Double Team Turnover! |vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima |vaen=Tom Wayland |desc=Bronzor made a brief appearance in ''[[DP097|Double Team Turnover!]]'' when it assisted Mars in her attempt to retrieve the Lustrous Orb from {{TRT}} before it was knocked out by {{AP|Staravia|Staraptor}}, {{TP|Dawn|Piplup}} and {{TP|Cynthia|Garchomp}}. Bronzor's only known move is {{m|Hypnosis}}.}} ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color=E6525A|bordercolor=F69C7B |ja=田中理恵 ''[[Rie Tanaka]]'' |en=[[Lisa Ortiz]] |ar=ميرنا الحسيني ''Mirna Huseini'' ([[JNS01]]-[[JNS04]]) |cs=Nikola Votočková ([[DP096]]-[[DP097]], [[JNS01]]-[[JNS04]])
